channel.title: Tech Blog
channel.description: Cutting-edge insights into technology, AI, and development

item.title: Raised salary 3x in two years
item.description: Raised salary from $2,800 to $8,400 in two years by shifting from hourly coding to fixed-scope fractional CTO contracts, cutting latency from 210 ms to 48 ms, and using Neon Postgres and Fly.io to cut AWS costs 78 %. For engineers who want to price like a team, not an individual.

item.title: Run local LLMs on a laptop today
item.description: Run a 7B LLM locally on a laptop in under 3 seconds per response — VRAM limits, Q4_K_M quantization, and the flags that actually work. Benchmarks on M2 Air, RTX 3060, and RTX 3080 included.

item.title: Rewrite your backend for 5G users or lose half your
item.description: Reduce 5G backend failures by 68% with a 120-line shim, latency budgets, and jittered retries. Learn what to measure first and which dials actually move the needle.

item.title: Ace remote interviews without a CS degree
item.description: Pass remote interviews without a CS degree — real system failures, trade-offs, and the $200/month tools that actually work

item.title: Vector search: when to use it — and when not to
item.description: Cut API response time by 65% with pgvector vs Milvus — 12 ms vs 4 ms p95, $0.01 vs $0.04 per 100 k queries, and when to ignore the dedicated vector database.

item.title: GitHub Actions costs 3x CircleCI at 50k builds
item.description: At 50k builds/month GitHub Actions costs $5k–$7k while CircleCI costs $1.8k–$2.2k — concrete egress, concurrency, and secrets numbers from three Nairobi fintech systems

item.title: RAG pipelines: what production breaks first
item.description: Cut RAG pipeline cost 9x and hit 12k QPS with 138ms P99 latency on 8 vCPUs — connection pooling, cache tiers, and Qdrant bloom filters made the difference. Learn what tutorials skip about production RAG.

item.title: Which AI IDE cuts your bill: Claude Code or Cursor
item.description: Cursor burned $86/month per developer on idle AWS while Claude Code stayed at $12 — here’s the exact experiment, benchmarks, and cost breakdown

item.title: Choose Arc over Toptal for African devs in 2026
item.description: Cut hiring time by 75% and save $4,000/month for African tech teams: Arc delivered candidates in 12 days vs 45 days on Andela and 28 days on Toptal in 2026.

item.title: Why European startups hire East African engineers
item.description: Cut support response time from 12h to 2.5h by fixing timezone overlap with East African engineers — overlap windows, UTC storage, and DST-proof scheduling with exact timings.

item.title: Got a remote tech job: what actually worked
item.description: Build one project that survives 100 users and 256 MB memory: the real signals that got me a remote job after 6 rejections

item.title: Big tech exits: what engineers actually quit for
item.description: Cut ‘ghost contributor’ rate by 75% with a 30-line service — impact tracking, observability, and the hidden dependencies that drain ownership in big tech teams.

item.title: Agents in production: 5 things docs skip
item.description: Cut multi-agent system latency from 8.4s to 1.2s in production — state, rate limits, and tool timeouts teams miss in the docs

item.title: 6 free AI tools that replaced $2k of my paid stack
item.description: Cut monthly tool costs by $819 using 6 free AI replacements — measured latency, quality deltas, and edge cases that actually matter to teams shipping in constrained environments.

item.title: Shift security left 3x faster without slowing deploys
item.description: Cut security block time 93% by moving scans to pre-commit and enforcing policy-as-code — no build slowdown, fewer false positives, and $35k saved in rework.

item.title: Choose wrong: FastAPI vs Django REST in prod
item.description: Cut API latency 4x and AWS costs 43% by choosing the right Python backend — FastAPI vs Django REST pros, cons, and real production numbers.

item.title: Doctors, lawyers, scientists: how they ship production
item.description: Cut p99 latency 60% by letting doctors, lawyers, and scientists build their own production modules — domainctl, Rust policies, and the hidden failure modes they’ll hit first.

item.title: AI changed senior devs: 9 tools reshaping 2026
item.description: Cut story-point estimation by 42% and incident MTTR by 65% with these 9 AI tools — real benchmarks, costs, and the mistakes that break them. The new senior skill in 2026 isn’t writing for-loops; it’s auditing AI outputs.

item.title: Win $4k remote jobs: Next.js + Supabase stack
item.description: Land $4k/month remote jobs using Next.js + Supabase: latency under 50ms, $4.56/month hosting, and the exact stack teams in Nairobi and Lagos use to sign contracts.

item.title: Async Python: when it helps, when it hurts
item.description: Cut API response time 64% with async Python — when async helps, when it freezes your event loop, real benchmarks and fixes
